title: Sequences
learning_objectives: Identify arithmetic (constant difference) vs geometric (constant ratio) sequences.
Use the arithmetic nth-term formula a_n = a_1 + (n − 1)d.
Use the geometric nth-term formula a_n = a_1 · r^(n−1).
Find the sum of an arithmetic sequence with (n/2)(first + last).

pedagogical_model.sequence: I do → we do → you do

items.prompt: Why is a formula faster than listing to find the 100th term of 3, 7, 11, …?
items.answer: A formula jumps straight to term 100 instead of writing 99 terms first.

retrieval_starter.purpose: Retrieves the difference/ratio distinction and the need for a direct formula — exactly what the nth-term rules provide today.
retrieval_starter.if_students_struggle: If difference vs ratio blurs, ask 'add or multiply to advance?' — add = arithmetic, multiply = geometric.

opening.prior_knowledge_bridge: A sequence is a function whose input is the term number n. You spotted the step rule in the Do-Now; today you turn that rule into a formula so you can leap to the 20th or 100th term without listing. Arithmetic adds a fixed d; geometric multiplies by a fixed r — same idea, different operation.
opening.learning_intention: By the end you can find any term of an arithmetic or geometric sequence with its formula and sum an arithmetic sequence.
success_criteria: I can apply a_n = a_1 + (n − 1)d for arithmetic sequences.
I can apply a_n = a_1 · r^(n−1) for geometric sequences.
I can sum an arithmetic sequence with (n/2)(first + last).

opening_script.move: Check; sort each Do-Now sequence as arithmetic or geometric.
Bridge: term number is the input; derive the (n−1) multiplier.

i_do.stem: In the arithmetic sequence 4, 7, 10, 13, …, what is the 20th term?
think_aloud_steps: First term a_1 = 4; common difference d = 7 − 4 = 3.
i_do.key_takeaway: Arithmetic nth term: a_n = a_1 + (n − 1)d. The jump count is (n − 1), not n.

we_do.stem: Find the 10th term of the arithmetic sequence with a_1 = 5 and d = 4.
In the geometric sequence 3, 6, 12, 24, …, what is the next term after 24?
scaffold_hints: Find the ratio between consecutive terms: 6/3 = 2.
Geometric sequences multiply by r each step.

immediate_feedback.if_incorrect: This sequence multiplies (×2), it doesn't add a constant.

you_do.stem: The first term of a geometric sequence is 5 and the 4th term is 40. What is the common ratio?
An arithmetic sequence has a_1 = 7 and a_5 = 23. What is the common difference?
What is the sum of the first 10 terms of the arithmetic sequence 3, 7, 11, …?

sample_held_out_items.stem: Find the 8th term of the arithmetic sequence with a_1 = 10 and d = −2.
In a geometric sequence, a_1 = 2 and r = 3. What is a_4?

mastery_check.definition: A topic is mastered at >=85% correct on new/held-out-style questions across >=2 spaced sessions with delay (PRD Section 9.2). Flashcard recall alone is not mastery; application under mixed, delayed conditions is the bar.
conditions: >=85% correct on new/held-out-style items (not flashcard recall) per session
sustained across >=2 spaced sessions with a delay between them
per-topic memory surfaced only after >=20 graded reviews (PRD give-up rule)
per-topic performance surfaced only after >=8 application attempts (PRD give-up rule)
mastery_check.on_fail: Not yet: route every miss to the required error log (tag why: content gap / reasoning flaw / timing / careless / trap), schedule spaced re-practice via topic-aware scheduling, then re-enter the we-do -> you-do loop before re-testing.
